_comment: Naming convention: <surface>_<element>_<role>. Surface = where the string appears (account_setup, settings, compose, …). Element = which UI thing inside that surface. Role = label / placeholder / hint / error / button / etc. Keep keys snake_case so they look good in autocomplete and don't fight ESLint identifier rules. Add new keys when you touch a string; the codebase is migrated lazily — see CLAUDE.md → i18n.
settings_general_language_hint: Change which language Nimbus Mail uses for its UI. Restart isn't required — the change applies live.
settings_general_language_auto_hint: Use the language reported by your operating system. Turn off to pin a specific language regardless of system setting.
account_setup_welcome_subtitle: Let's set up your email account
account_setup_account_name_hint: How this account is labelled inside Nimbus.
account_setup_your_name_hint: Shown as the sender on outgoing mail. Defaults to the account name when empty.
account_setup_imap_explainer: IMAP is the protocol used to receive your emails. Port 993 uses TLS encryption (recommended).
account_setup_password_hint: Stored securely in your OS keychain — never written to disk in plain text.
account_setup_smtp_explainer: SMTP is the protocol used to send your emails. Port 587 uses STARTTLS encryption (recommended).
account_setup_jmap_hint: Modern push-based mail protocol. Only enable if your provider supports it.
account_setup_signature_hint: Appended to new messages sent from this account. You can change it later in Settings.
account_setup_validation_step0: Please fill in the account name and email address.
account_setup_validation_step1: Please enter your IMAP server hostname and password.
account_setup_validation_step2: Please enter your SMTP server hostname.
account_setup_discovery_hint_fallback: Couldn't auto-discover {domain} — best-guess hostnames filled in. Edit if needed.
account_setup_cert_title: The server's TLS certificate isn't trusted by default.
account_setup_cert_explainer: This is normal for self-hosted mail servers using a self-signed certificate. Compare the fingerprint below with your server's actual certificate before trusting.
account_setup_cert_probe_failed: Could not retrieve the server certificate to display: {reason}
account_setup_cert_trusting_one: Trusting 1 self-signed certificate for this account.
account_setup_cert_trusting_many: Trusting {n} self-signed certificates for this account.
